metadata.title: Agent Battle: When Prompt Engineering Becomes a Sport.

skin_warnings: B06: palette=claude but the outro is 'LogoOutro' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · Agent Battle: When Prompt Engineering Becomes a Sport.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Agents connect outbound to an event server that tracks each one's diamond count on a live leaderboard. Each agent has four levers: model, system prompt, skills, and MCP tools. The score is diamonds mined, with fewest tokens used as the tiebreaker. That tiebreaker makes token efficiency a first-class competitive goal, not just good practice.

artifactLines: Agents connect outbound to an event server that tracks each one's diamond count on a live leaderboard.
Each agent has four levers: model, system prompt, skills, and MCP tools.
The score is diamonds mined, with fewest tokens used as the tiebreaker.
That tiebreaker makes token efficiency a first-class competitive goal, not just good practice.

beats.narration_text: Here is why Agent Battle: When Prompt Engineering Becomes a Sport. belongs in your working knowledge. The systems, policies, and tools shaped by it affect decisions at your level — whether or not you understand the mechanism. Knowing the underlying logic gives you the frame; missing it means reasoning from output with no access to the inputs.

artifactLines: Agents connect outbound to an event server that tracks each one's diamond count on a live leaderboard.
Each agent has four levers: model, system prompt, skills, and MCP tools.
The score is diamonds mined, with fewest tokens used as the tiebreaker.
That tiebreaker makes token efficiency a first-class competitive goal, not just good practice.

beats.narration_text: Making token efficiency the tiebreaker turns prompt engineering into an actual sport, and I want to build a competitive agent for this. Given the four levers — model, system prompt, skills, and MCP tools — with diamonds-mined scoring and fewest-tokens as the tiebreaker, can you (1) draft a tight system prompt that wouldn't lose on the tiebreaker; (2) name three skills as callable Python functions my agent would need to mine diamonds; (3) explain how I'd decide when a bigger model is worth its extra token cost?

props.topic: HUMANITARIANS AI · BUILD WITH CLAUDE · ANTHROPIC
props.segment: Agent Battle: When Prompt Engineering Becomes a Sport.
props.command: Making token efficiency the tiebreaker turns prompt engineering into an actual sport, and I want to build a competitive agent for this. Given the four levers — model, system prompt, skills, and MCP tools — with diamonds-mined scoring and fewest-tokens as the tiebreaker, can you (1) draft a tight system prompt that wouldn't lose on the tiebreaker; (2) name three skills as callable Python functions my agent would need to mine diamonds; (3) explain how I'd decide when a bigger model is worth its extra token cost?

beats.narration_text: Setup is deliberately simple. Your agent connects outbound to the event server — no tunnels, no ports, just an API key. The server tracks every agent's diamond count on a live leaderboard. All agents run simultaneously in the same world. Resource conflicts are real. The game is adversarial.

graphic.production_viz: Network diagram: agent -> event server. Leaderboard on right. Multiple agents shown as competing nodes.

beats.narration_text: Your agent has four configuration levers. Model: which Claude variant to run. System prompt: the agent's strategy, persona, and decision-making framework. Skills: callable Python functions — your agent's action vocabulary. MCP tools: additional capabilities from external servers. The competition score is a direct readout of how well you've tuned these four.

graphic.production_viz: Four labeled boxes: Model / System Prompt / Skills / MCP Tools. Arrows to score bar.

beats.narration_text: The scoring mechanic is diamonds mined. The tiebreaker is fewest tokens used. This makes token efficiency a first-class competitive goal — not just good practice. A bloated system prompt that mines the same diamonds as a tight one loses on the tiebreaker. Prompt engineering is now measurable, comparable, and ranked.

graphic.production_viz: Scoreboard: diamonds (primary) + tokens (tiebreaker). Two agents same diamonds — fewer tokens wins.

beats.narration_text: Ask Claude: 'Write a system prompt for a Minecraft diamond-mining agent — maximize diamond yield while minimizing token usage per action.' That's the actual competition constraint. What strategy does Claude devise when token efficiency is a hard requirement? Run it. Kore, in for Humanitarians AI.
